title: Crossrail (Elizabeth Line)
description: A major new railway for London and the South East.
statusRationale: Project complete.

evidence.title: Crossrail Completion Report
evidence.description: Final project report for the Elizabeth Line.

title: High Speed 2 (HS2) Phase 1
description: A new high-capacity, high-speed railway connecting London and the West Midlands, via the existing West Coast Main Line.
statusRationale: Delivery confidence is low; the project is being re-baselined after scope reductions (Network North) and cost estimate updates.

evidence.title: HS2 Official Website
evidence.description: Official information and updates on HS2.

title: High Speed 2 (HS2) Phase 2b / Northern Powerhouse Rail
description: Extending high-speed rail in the North and Midlands, integrating HS2 Phase 2b with the Northern Powerhouse Rail network to improve east–west and north–south connectivity.
statusRationale: Project realigned to the Integrated Rail Plan; delivery timeline extended to mid-2040s in line with target year. Benefits have been revised down due to scope changes.

title: East West Rail – Connection Stage 1
description: The East West Rail (EWR) scheme will create a new rail link from Oxford to Cambridge:contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}. Connection Stage 1 delivers the section between Oxford, Bicester, Milton Keynes and Bedford.
statusRationale: Delivery is feasible but faces schedule and funding challenges as construction progresses.

title: East West Rail – Connection Stages 2 & 3
description: Later phases of East West Rail extending the new Oxford–Cambridge railway from Bedford through to Cambridge:contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}.
statusRationale: Successful delivery appears unachievable under current plans, pending funding and route approval for the Bedford–Cambridge sections.

title: Lower Thames Crossing
description: A proposed new dual–carriageway road and tunnel under the River Thames east of London, connecting Kent and Essex:contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}.
statusRationale: Awaiting planning consent; project timeline and cost under review but still progressing through design and enabling works.

title: A303 Amesbury to Berwick Down (Stonehenge Tunnel)
description: Upgrade of the A303 near Stonehenge to a dual carriageway, including a new twin-bore tunnel under the World Heritage Site.
statusRationale: Project remains on hold pending conclusion of legal challenges to its planning approval, introducing uncertainty to cost and schedule.

title: A12 Chelmsford to A120 Widening
description: Widening the A12 to three lanes between Chelmsford (junction 19) and the A120 interchange (junction 25) in Essex, to increase capacity and reduce congestion.
statusRationale: Legal challenge to the project’s Development Consent Order is creating schedule uncertainty, although all development milestones to date were achieved.

title: A66 Northern Trans-Pennine Upgrade
description: Upgrading the remaining six single-carriageway sections of the A66 between the A1(M) at Scotch Corner and the M6 at Penrith to dual carriageway, including key junction improvements.
statusRationale: Development Consent was granted in March 2024. Final business case and start of works are pending, with assurance reviews planned to manage remaining risks.

title: A428 Black Cat to Caxton Gibbet
description: Construction of a new 10-mile dual carriageway between the Black Cat roundabout (A1) in Bedfordshire and Caxton Gibbet roundabout (A428) in Cambridgeshire, including a new junction at Black Cat.
statusRationale: Full business case approved and construction started in late 2023, improving delivery confidence.

title: A417 "Air Balloon" Improvement
description: Upgrading the A417 in Gloucestershire by building a new dual carriageway to replace a winding single-lane stretch near Birdlip (the "Air Balloon"), improving safety and connectivity.
statusRationale: Work is underway ahead of schedule. An assurance review in late 2022 rated the project Green, with all recommendations addressed.

title: Transpennine Route Upgrade (TRU)
description: Major rail upgrade between York, Leeds and Manchester to electrify the route and enhance capacity, speed and reliability across the Transpennine corridor.
statusRationale: Facing cost and schedule pressures due to scope increases and high inflation:contentReference[oaicite:18]{index=18}, but project delivery continues with phased works underway.

title: East Coast Digital Programme
description: Introduction of in-cab digital signaling (European Train Control System) on the southern East Coast Main Line from London King’s Cross to south of Grantham, replacing lineside signals with modern train-to-track communication.
statusRationale: Innovative first-of-kind deployment in UK rail—delivery is feasible but requires managing industry-wide change and technical integration risks.

title: Midland Main Line Electrification (Phase 3)
description: Electrification and upgrade of the Midland Main Line north of Kettering, extending electric train operation towards Nottingham, Derby and Sheffield to increase capacity and cut journey times.
statusRationale: Delivery is underway in stages. Recent cost pressures and schedule adjustments keep the project in an Amber status as work progresses.

title: Sizewell C New Nuclear Power Station
description: Proposed 3.2 GW nuclear power plant in Suffolk (twin EPR reactors) developed by EDF and the UK government to provide low-carbon electricity and support energy security.
statusRationale: Delivery confidence information is commercially sensitive and has been withheld:contentReference[oaicite:21]{index=21}. Government negotiations on financing and contracts are ongoing.

title: Carbon Capture, Usage and Storage (CCUS) Clusters Programme
description: A programme to deliver industrial-scale carbon capture and storage in at least two UK industrial clusters by the mid-2020s and two further clusters by 2030:contentReference[oaicite:22]{index=22}, capturing 20–30 million tonnes CO₂ annually by 2030.
statusRationale: Delivery confidence details are withheld for commercial reasons:contentReference[oaicite:23]{index=23}. The programme is proceeding with Track-1 cluster projects and further cluster selections pending funding decisions.

title: Geological Disposal Facility (GDF) for Radioactive Waste
description: A nationally significant infrastructure programme to identify a willing host community and construct a deep Geological Disposal Facility for the permanent, safe disposal of the UK’s higher-activity radioactive waste:contentReference[oaicite:24]{index=24}.
statusRationale: The DCA is Amber reflecting considerable uncertainty around site selection, geology and technical feasibility at this stage:contentReference[oaicite:25]{index=25}. Progress is being made with three Community Partnerships engaged in detailed evaluations.

title: Great British Nuclear (SMR Programme)
description: Establishment of Great British Nuclear (GBN) as a delivery body to develop new nuclear capacity, starting with a competitive selection of Small Modular Reactor technologies and preparation of sites for up to 24 GW of nuclear by 2050:contentReference[oaicite:26]{index=26}.
statusRationale: Delivery status is commercially sensitive (project in early procurement stages):contentReference[oaicite:27]{index=27}. The SMR technology competition and site identification are underway, but timelines depend on industry responses.

title: Green Heat Network Fund (GHNF)
description: A £288 million capital grant scheme to incentivise the transition of heating networks to low-carbon sources at scale, driving carbon savings and increased use of heat pumps, waste heat and other low-carbon heat in networks:contentReference[oaicite:28]{index=28}.
statusRationale: Additional funding in 2023 extended the programme through 2027/28, and delivery confidence improved from Amber to Green as the scheme’s pipeline and support systems have been strengthened:contentReference[oaicite:29]{index=29}.

title: Home Upgrade Grant & Local Authority Delivery (HULA) Schemes
description: Programmes providing grants to upgrade energy efficiency and low-carbon heating in fuel-poor homes in England. The Home Upgrade Grant (HUG) targets off-gas-grid homes, and the Local Authority Delivery (LAD) scheme (Phases 1–3) retrofitted low-performing homes via local councils:contentReference[oaicite:30]{index=30}:contentReference[oaicite:31]{index=31}.
statusRationale: Rated Amber due to under-delivery against initial targets in earlier phases (lower uptake and project delays):contentReference[oaicite:32]{index=32}. Programme adjustments (more support for local authorities, policy tweaks) are being implemented to improve delivery:contentReference[oaicite:33]{index=33}.

title: Social Housing Decarbonisation Fund (SHDF)
description: A £3.8 billion programme to retrofit England’s social housing stock to EPC Band C energy efficiency by 2030, delivered in waves (a £60m demonstrator, £179m Wave 1, £778m Wave 2.1, etc.) to upgrade hundreds of thousands of homes:contentReference[oaicite:34]{index=34}:contentReference[oaicite:35]{index=35}.
statusRationale: Wave 1 is concluding successfully, but future waves must accelerate installation rates to meet targets:contentReference[oaicite:36]{index=36}. An IPA review in March 2024 rated the upcoming Wave 3 Amber, reflecting these delivery challenges:contentReference[oaicite:37]{index=37}.

title: Spherical Tokamak for Energy Production (STEP)
description: A staged programme to design and build, by around 2040, a prototype fusion energy plant delivering net energy and demonstrating the commercial viability of fusion power:contentReference[oaicite:38]{index=38}. The STEP prototype will be a world-first, paving the way for a global fusion energy market.
statusRationale: An ambitious, high-risk research programme – the IPA’s delivery confidence remains Amber given the scale of technological challenges, even as early Tranche 1 objectives were met on schedule:contentReference[oaicite:39]{index=39}:contentReference[oaicite:40]{index=40}.

title: BEPPS2 (Sellafield Box Encapsulation Plant Product Store 2)
description: Construction of a modern intermediate-level radioactive waste storage facility at Sellafield. BEPPS2 will safely encapsulate and store ~7,470 packages of legacy ILW retrieved from older silos, with a design life of 100 years:contentReference[oaicite:41]{index=41}:contentReference[oaicite:42]{index=42}.
statusRationale: The project is on track against its approved baseline plan:contentReference[oaicite:43]{index=43}. No significant threats to the 2035 completion date have emerged, and delivery is proceeding within strategic tolerances.

title: Dreadnought Submarine Programme
description: Programme to design and build four new Dreadnought-class ballistic missile submarines to replace the Royal Navy’s Vanguard-class, sustaining the UK’s continuous at-sea nuclear deterrent into the 2030s and beyond:contentReference[oaicite:44]{index=44}.
statusRationale: The programme remains on track for HMS Dreadnought (first of class) to enter service in the early 2030s:contentReference[oaicite:45]{index=45}. However, it is inherently high-risk and complex; substantial schedule and technical contingencies are being managed (delivery confidence rated Amber).

title: Future Combat Air System (FCAS / Tempest)
description: International programme (UK, Italy, Japan) to develop a sixth-generation fighter aircraft system to replace the RAF Typhoon fleet from the mid-2030s:contentReference[oaicite:46]{index=46}:contentReference[oaicite:47]{index=47}. FCAS will include a new stealth combat aircraft and a suite of adjunct systems (such as drones and advanced networking).
statusRationale: Early-stage development: the tri-nation Global Combat Air Programme was launched in 2023. Delivery confidence is Amber, acknowledging the scale of the technological and management challenge to deliver a new fighter in the required timeframe:contentReference[oaicite:48]{index=48}.

title: Type 26 Frigate Programme
description: Construction of eight Type 26 "City-class" frigates for the Royal Navy, advanced anti-submarine warfare warships replacing the Type 23. Three ships (Batch 1) are under construction on the Clyde, with five Batch 2 to follow.
statusRationale: Shipbuilding is underway, but the programme has experienced schedule delays and cost growth due to design changes and supply chain challenges. Delivery confidence remains Amber as the first ships near completion.

title: Type 31 Frigate Programme
description: Programme to build five Type 31 "Inspiration-class" general purpose frigates in Rosyth for the Royal Navy, providing a capable and flexible warship at lower cost, with all five planned to be in service by 2030.
statusRationale: The programme is progressing well against an aggressive timeline. Construction of the lead ship is on track, and no major issues have been reported, yielding a Green delivery confidence status.

title: AJAX Armoured Fighting Vehicle Programme
description: Procurement of 589 Ajax armoured fighting vehicles for the British Army, intended to transform its armoured reconnaissance capability. The vehicles feature advanced sensors and weaponry on a tracked platform.
statusRationale: Significant performance problems (noise/vibration issues) halted trials for prolonged periods. As a result, the programme is severely delayed and over budget, with the IPA rating it Red (delivery of operational vehicles still uncertain).

title: Challenger 3 Main Battle Tank Upgrade
description: Life-Extension Programme to upgrade 148 Challenger 2 tanks to Challenger 3 standard, featuring a new turret with a 120mm smoothbore gun, improved sights, armour, engine and electronic architecture:contentReference[oaicite:49]{index=49}.
statusRationale: Upgrades are in early prototyping. The project is Amber due to tight delivery timescales and dependency on a single prime contractor. Recent cost increases (from £1.435 bn to £1.987 bn Whole Life Cost) reflect scope additions:contentReference[oaicite:50]{index=50}, which are being managed within revised budgets.

title: Skynet 6 MILSATCOM Programme
description: Next-generation military satellite communications system for UK forces, replacing Skynet 5. Involves new satellites (Skynet 6A and follow-ons) and ground infrastructure to provide secure beyond-line-of-sight communications worldwide.
statusRationale: One satellite (Skynet 6A) is under construction following some contracting delays. Overall schedule confidence is Amber as the MoD finalizes requirements and procurement for subsequent satellites and ground segment upgrades.

title: Clyde Submarine Infrastructure Programme
description: Major upgrade of HMNB Clyde (Faslane) facilities to support the Royal Navy’s entire submarine fleet at a single site. Includes new dock, engineering and training facilities to accommodate Dreadnought-class submarines and sustain Continuous At-Sea Deterrence:contentReference[oaicite:51]{index=51}.
statusRationale: This £1.8 billion infrastructure programme is complex and has little schedule float relative to Dreadnought submarine delivery. It is in the early construction phase with an Amber status, reflecting the need to closely manage construction risks to meet the required in-service date.

title: Emergency Services Network (ESN) / ESMCP
description: Home Office-led Emergency Services Mobile Communications Programme (ESMCP) to replace the Airwave system with a new 4G-based Emergency Services Network. ESN will provide mission-critical voice and data for police, fire and ambulance over an enhanced commercial 4G LTE network:contentReference[oaicite:52]{index=52}.
statusRationale: The IPA continues to rate ESN delivery as Red:contentReference[oaicite:53]{index=53}. While re-procurement of key contracts is in progress, the programme has repeatedly missed milestones and the transition from Airwave has been delayed indefinitely pending a viable revised plan.

title: Digital Services at the Border (DSAB)
description: A programme delivering new digital border systems to transform how UK Border Force and partner agencies operate. DSAB provides modern systems for border crossings, visa processing, security checks and data sharing, replacing legacy IT:contentReference[oaicite:54]{index=54}.
statusRationale: Core releases have gone live, but some planned capabilities have been descoped or delayed. The Amber rating reflects schedule slippage and the need to integrate with other Home Office immigration and border systems still in development.

title: GOV.UK One Login Programme
description: A cross-government programme to implement a single digital identity and login system for all UK government services. One Login will allow citizens to prove their identity once and access multiple online services securely, replacing older systems like GOV.UK Verify:contentReference[oaicite:55]{index=55}.
statusRationale: The programme is progressing, with 30 services integrated and 4.1 million users onboarded:contentReference[oaicite:56]{index=56}. However, it faces significant technical complexity and resource challenges; the IPA has flagged multiple delivery risks (hence an Amber status).

title: New Hospital Programme (40 Hospitals by 2030)
description: A flagship DHSC programme to deliver 40 new hospitals across England by 2030, involving a standardised “Hospital 2.0” design and modular construction approach, plus major rebuilds of aging hospital facilities:contentReference[oaicite:57]{index=57}.
statusRationale: The IPA has rated the New Hospital Programme as Red:contentReference[oaicite:58]{index=58} – successful delivery appears unachievable as originally planned. Project delays, cost inflation, and scope changes mean later phases are at high risk of not being delivered by 2030 without significant replanning and additional funding.

title: Prison Estate Capacity Programme
description: Ministry of Justice programme to create thousands of new prison places by building new prisons and expanding existing ones. Includes construction of modern prisons (e.g. HMP Five Wells, HMP Fosse Way) and additional houseblocks to address overcrowding and future demand.
statusRationale: The programme is delivering new facilities but has faced planning and procurement delays. Several new prisons are under construction, but the overall schedule to add 20,000 prison places by the mid-2020s remains challenging (Amber status).

title: HMCTS Court Reform Programme
description: A transformation programme to modernise the courts and tribunals of England and Wales through digitisation, modern case management IT, and rationalisation of the court estate. It aims to streamline processes and improve access to justice by moving services online.
statusRationale: Delivery has been delayed, and scope reduced in areas such as online criminal proceedings. While many digital services have been implemented, the overall programme is rated Amber due to user adoption challenges and the need for further legislative changes to fully realize benefits.

title: Thames Tideway Tunnel
description: A 25-km "super-sewer" tunnel under London’s River Thames to capture and store combined sewage overflows, preventing pollution. The Tideway Tunnel will run from Acton to Beckton, largely following the Thames, and work with the Lee Tunnel to modernise London’s Victorian sewer system.
statusRationale: Now over 90% complete, the project is on course to be operational by around 2025. It remains Amber due to residual construction risks and the complex system commissioning phase, but overall is progressing within its revised schedule and budget:contentReference[oaicite:59]{index=59}.

evidence.title: Tideway Construction Update
evidence.summary: Construction is progressing with some delays due to supply chain issues.

title: Customs Declaration Service (CDS)
description: HMRC’s IT system replacement for handling import/export customs declarations. CDS replaces the old CHIEF system to accommodate post-Brexit customs volumes and more complex requirements, providing a modern platform for traders to submit declarations.
statusRationale: CDS has successfully onboarded the vast majority of traders (import declarations fully migrated in 2022). Delivery is essentially complete with remaining functionality (exports) on track, hence a Green status.

title: Cambridge South Railway Station
description: A new rail station serving the Cambridge Biomedical Campus and southern Cambridge, providing additional capacity on the West Anglia Main Line.
statusRationale: Works under way following Development Consent Order granted in December 2023.

title: Greater Manchester Bee Network
description: Integrated transport system for Greater Manchester, combining bus franchising, Metrolink, active-travel routes and simplified ticketing under a unified ‘Bee Network’ brand.
statusRationale: Bus franchising launched in 2023; staged expansion and infrastructure build-out continues through the decade.

title: York Central Regeneration
description: A 45-hectare brownfield regeneration scheme behind York rail station, delivering new homes, offices, public realm and transport links.
statusRationale: Infrastructure works funded and under way; first development plots marketed in 2024.

title: Bristol Temple Quarter & St Philip’s Marsh Redevelopment
description: Major regeneration of 130 ha around Bristol Temple Meads station, including mixed-use development, new university campus and station expansion.
statusRationale: Infrastructure funding agreement signed 2023; phased delivery through 2030s.

title: Birmingham Eastside Metro Extension
description: An extension of the West Midlands Metro from Bull Street to Digbeth, linking the city centre with the forthcoming HS2 Curzon Street station.
statusRationale: Utility diversions complete; track-laying began in 2024 with opening targeted for 2027.

title: Northumberland Line Reopening
description: Reinstatement of passenger services between Ashington, Blyth and Newcastle, with six new or rebuilt stations and track upgrades.
statusRationale: Main construction contracts awarded 2023; testing and commissioning scheduled late-2024.

title: Plymouth & South Devon Freeport
description: Freeport programme creating tax-advantaged sites at Oceansgate, Langage and Sherford to drive marine, defence and clean-energy investment.
statusRationale: Government approval granted December 2022; site infrastructure works under way.

title: Leeds Integrated Station Masterplan
description: Comprehensive remodelling of Leeds rail station, adding capacity, new entrances and urban realm, integrated with mass-transit proposals.
statusRationale: Masterplan endorsed 2024; GRIP 3 design phase and funding negotiations in progress.

title: Nottingham Broad Marsh Transformation
description: Redevelopment of the former Broad Marsh shopping centre into a mixed-use district with green public realm, new library, homes and office space.
statusRationale: Demolition complete; enabling works for ‘Green Heart’ park commenced 2024 with phased build-out to 2028.

title: Hoo Peninsula Infrastructure Programme
description: Package of transport and environmental schemes enabling strategic housing growth on the Hoo Peninsula in Medway.
statusRationale: Housing Infrastructure Fund grant confirmed 2021; business cases under review following cost increases.

title: International Advanced Manufacturing Park (IAMP)
description: A 150-hectare manufacturing park on the Sunderland–South Tyneside boundary, creating thousands of jobs in the automotive and renewable-energy supply chains.
statusRationale: Phase 1 infrastructure complete; further plots and Nissan EV battery gigafactory under build through 2027.

title: Mid-Cornwall Metro
description: Rail enhancements linking Newquay, St Austell and Falmouth with through services, new rolling stock and signalling upgrades to create a ‘metro’ frequency.
statusRationale: Awarded £50 m Levelling Up Fund in 2023; detailed design and timetable planning in progress.

title: A350 Melksham Bypass
description: Construction of a new 9 km dual carriageway bypass east of Melksham to relieve congestion on the A350 strategic route.
statusRationale: Preferred route announced 2023; statutory consultation scheduled for late-2025 before DCO submission.

title: Teesside Freeport & Teesworks Infrastructure
description: Delivery of roads, quays and utilities to unlock the 1 800-acre Teesworks site, supporting carbon capture, hydrogen and offshore-wind fabrication clusters.
statusRationale: Seed capital from UK Infrastructure Bank and Levelling Up Fund; new South Bank Quay operational 2024, further works ongoing.

title: Sturry Link Road, Canterbury
description: New highway and viaduct over the railway and River Stour to remove a key bottleneck on the A28 and unlock housing growth at Sturry.
statusRationale: Construction started spring 2023; bridge beams due to be installed in 2024.

title: Durham Bus Station Redevelopment
description: Replacement of Durham’s 1970s bus station with a modern interchange and improved public realm to increase capacity and accessibility.
statusRationale: Main contractor appointed 2023; structural frame rising with completion targeted 2026.

title: South Wokingham Distributor Road
description: Four-phase highway scheme creating a spine road through the South Wokingham strategic development area and new rail bridge at Montague Park.
statusRationale: Phases 1–3 complete; final eastern section and bridge funded via Homes England and due 2026.

title: Exeter Science Park Expansion
description: Additional laboratories, offices and a new mobility hub at Exeter Science Park to support hi-tech growth in the Exeter and East Devon Growth Point.
statusRationale: Levelling Up funding awarded 2024; detailed design progressing for start on site 2025.

title: Carlisle Southern Link Road
description: An 8 km bypass south of Carlisle linking the A595 and M6, unlocking the St Cuthbert’s Garden Village housing development.
statusRationale: Construction began January 2023; bridge works over River Caldew in progress.

title: Sheffield Supertram Renewals Programme
description: Comprehensive track, power and systems renewal of the Sheffield Supertram network to secure its long-term future and enable possible extensions.
statusRationale: Government funding agreement in principle (CRSTS) 2022; procurement and detailed design under way with staged renewals through the 2020s.

title: East Leeds Orbital Route – Finishing Works
description: Final landscaping, active‑travel links and smart‑traffic systems on the new dual‑carriageway orbital route connecting the A6120 to J46 of the M1.
statusRationale: Major civils complete; snagging and landscaping underway for hand‑over in late 2024.

title: Armley Gyratory Remodelling – Phase 2
description: Replacement of footbridges, new segregated cycleways and final carriageway widening at the key junction west of Leeds city centre.
statusRationale: Bridge fabrication delays have pushed programme by four months.

title: Leeds City Square Gateway
description: Pedestrianisation and public‑realm transformation outside Leeds railway station, removing through‑traffic and creating event space and greenery.
statusRationale: Surfacing and planting continue; completion due autumn 2024 ahead of Year of Culture activities.

title: White Rose Rail Station (Leeds)
description: New two‑platform rail station on the Leeds–Huddersfield line serving the White Rose business park and the Elland Road corridor.
statusRationale: Platform installation on programme; overhead line work coordination poses schedule risk.

title: Leeds Bradford Airport Parkway Station
description: Parkway rail station and 350‑space park‑and‑ride to improve public‑transport connections to Leeds Bradford Airport.
statusRationale: Funding gap following Network Rail cost review; alternative options being explored.

title: Aire Park Regeneration – Phase 1
description: Delivery of new public parkland, offices and 1,100 homes on the former Tetley Brewery South Bank site.
statusRationale: Ground remediation complete; vertical build due to start Q1 2025 subject to market conditions.

title: South Bank Viaduct Active‑Travel Greenway
description: Conversion of the redundant Holbeck Viaduct into an elevated walking and cycling route linking the South Bank to Elland Road.
statusRationale: Concept design funded; full scheme dependent on securing Levelling‑Up resources.

title: Leeds PIPES District Heating Network – Phase 2
description: Extension of the Leeds PIPES low‑carbon heat network to the South Bank and through‑routing across the city centre.
statusRationale: Pipe‑laying works running ahead of schedule with minimal traffic disruption.

title: Leeds CityBike Docked Hire Scheme
description: Roll‑out of 630 e‑bikes across 140 docking stations serving inner‑ring neighbourhoods and university campuses.
statusRationale: Supplier appointed; first 50 docks operational since May 2024.

title: Bus & HGV Retrofit – Leeds Clean Air Programme
description: Retrofit of 400 buses and 600 HGVs with Euro VI technology to reduce NOx emissions following cancellation of the charging CAZ.
statusRationale: 75 % of eligible fleet now upgraded; final tranche scheduled winter 2024/25.

title: Elland New Rail Station
description: Two‑platform station on the Calder Valley Line with pedestrian links to Elland town centre and Park Road.
statusRationale: Design approval stage; full business case submission planned mid‑2025.

evidence.title: Elland Rail Station project takes another step forward
evidence.summary: Progress on the new Elland Rail Station has taken a major step forward after a contractor was appointed to finalize the project’s detailed design. On-site surveys are underway and a full business case will follow by mid-2025, paving the way for final approval and the start of construction.

evidence.title: Elland Rail Station Moves Forward: West Yorkshire’s £25m Transport Boost on Track
evidence.summary: Plans for the new £25 million Elland Rail Station are making significant progress, with completion now projected for late 2026. Contractor Keltbray is aiming to finish the final design stage by summer 2024, after which the West Yorkshire Combined Authority will review the full business case to move the project into the construction phase.

evidence.title: New train station moves a step closer
evidence.summary: A contractor has been appointed to oversee the final development stages of Elland’s new railway station, which will be added to the Calder Valley Line and is now expected to open in 2026. Keltbray Infrastructure Services will complete detailed design work by next summer, after which the project can proceed to final approval and then construction, according to local officials.

title: A641 Bradford–Brighouse–Huddersfield Corridor Programme
description: Series of junction upgrades, bus‑priority and active‑travel measures along the A641 cross‑boundary strategic route.
statusRationale: Outline business case approved; phased construction to follow between 2026‑30.

title: Brighouse Town Deal – Canalside & Market Hub
description: Redevelopment of Brighouse Open Market and creation of new public spaces on the canalside and Thornton Square.
statusRationale: Main works started; market relocation underway; completion expected late 2025.

evidence.title: On the move – Brighouse’s market prepares for temporary home
evidence.summary: Brighouse Open Market is relocating to a temporary site this autumn so that work can begin on constructing its new permanent market hall on the canalside as part of the £19.1 million Town Deal. After planning permission was secured in March 2024, Triton Construction was appointed as contractor for the market redevelopment, with the main construction phase set to start by late September 2024 and last around 12 months.

evidence.title: Milestone moment for Brighouse as Town Deal market transformation begins
evidence.summary: By late 2024, construction was underway on Brighouse’s Town Deal projects to revitalize the waterfront and market area. Demolition of the old canalside market building began in October, and West Yorkshire-based Triton Construction has been tasked with building the new covered market hall as a focal point for the town’s regeneration.

evidence.title: Brighouse Town Deal: Progress made on £19.1 million Government-funded projects in 2024 including Brighouse Market
evidence.summary: According to a year-end 2024 update, Brighouse’s Town Deal scheme has reached key milestones in both the market and public realm projects. The old market site was cleared and construction of a modern market hall (with 21 fixed stalls and improved facilities) is in progress, on track for completion in late 2025. Meanwhile, £6 million of the Town Deal is dedicated to Canalside and Thornton Square improvements – creating a new waterfront public space and enhancing the town centre’s image to encourage recreation and community gatherings.

title: Hebden Bridge Flood Alleviation Scheme
description: Combination of walls, raised footpaths and movable weirs along the Hebden Water and River Calder.
statusRationale: Ground‑investigation results delayed; revised programme under review.

title: Todmorden Learning Centre & Enterprise Hub Phase 2
description: Low‑carbon retrofit of a Grade II‑listed former college to create co‑working and skills spaces focused on green technologies.
statusRationale: Fabric upgrade package 80 % complete; fit‑out to follow.

title: Calderdale Full‑Fibre Spine Extension
description: installation of 38 km of gigabit fibre spine linking council assets and rural communities.
statusRationale: Civil trenching 95 % complete; testing and commissioning scheduled November 2024.

title: Calderdale Active‑Travel Package 2024‑26
description: Delivery of 23 km of segregated cycle routes connecting Sowerby Bridge, Halifax and Brighouse.
statusRationale: Utility diversions causing localised delays.

title: Dewsbury Market Redevelopment
description: Comprehensive rebuild and re‑roofing of the Grade II‑listed open market, creating food hall, stalls and events space.
statusRationale: Cost inflation risk being managed through value‑engineering.

title: Dewsbury Blueprint – Station Gateway
description: Public‑realm and transport interchange improvements linking Dewsbury rail station to the town centre arcade.
statusRationale: Main works contract awarded April 2024; construction mobilised.

title: Ravensthorpe Station Relocation & Grade‑Separation
description: Building a new station 300 m east and grade‑separating lines to enable faster Transpennine services.
statusRationale: Network Rail main works now let; enabling works progressing.

title: Spen Valley Leisure Centre Renewable Retrofit
description: Installation of ground‑source heat pumps and solar PV to achieve net‑zero operation at the new leisure centre in Liversedge.
statusRationale: Drilling works underway; system commissioning due March 2025.

title: A629 Corridor – Phase 4 Ainley Top & Ainley Bridge
description: Reconfiguration of Ainley Top roundabout and dedicated bus‑only link towards Halifax to remove bottlenecks.
statusRationale: Consultation launched January 2025; business case in development.

title: Holmfirth Town Centre Access Scheme
description: Highway realignment, new bridge and riverside walkway to reduce congestion and enhance public space in Holmfirth.
statusRationale: Funding confirmed via Levelling Up Fund Round 2; detailed design ongoing.

title: Huddersfield Southern Gateway (A62 Smart Corridor)
description: Intelligent traffic‑management, bus‑priority and cycle superhighway on the A62 Leeds Road into Huddersfield.
statusRationale: Signal‑equipment shortages causing procurement delays.

evidence.title: On the right track: A62 Leeds Road Smart Corridor making progress and tipped to deliver on time
evidence.summary: Kirklees Council reported that construction on the A62 Leeds Road “Smart Corridor” was progressing well in late 2022, with the main works on track to be completed by spring 2023. The scheme – part of West Yorkshire’s Corridor Improvement Programme – is designed to cut congestion and improve journey times with new bus lanes, cycle facilities and junction upgrades along the route.

evidence.title: Brand new £8m Yorkshire road dug up weeks later due to gas leak
evidence.summary: The £7.9 million A62 Leeds Road Smart Corridor scheme in Huddersfield reached completion in mid-April 2023, delivering major highway upgrades to tackle congestion and improve travel times and safety on this key route. (The project’s final resurfacing was finished in April, slightly later than originally planned due to earlier schedule adjustments and supply challenges.)

evidence.title: Updates to schedule of upcoming A62 improvements will mean less disruption for residents and businesses
evidence.summary: In 2022, the council revised the construction schedule for the A62 Smart Corridor project to accelerate works and reduce disruption. An intensified seven-week closure at a key junction was implemented so that overall roadworks could finish by early December 2022 – about two months sooner than originally planned. (Subsequent updates indicated the project’s final completion date slipped into spring 2023, partly due to challenges in obtaining new traffic signal equipment.)

title: Penistone Line Upgrade (Huddersfield‑Sheffield)
description: Track, signalling and station improvements to enable half‑hourly services and increase line‑speed.
statusRationale: Awaiting DfT decision on Restoring Your Railway funding.

title: Kirklees Municipal Full‑Fibre Roll‑Out
description: Connection of 210 public‑sector sites to gigabit‑capable fibre as part of the DCMS LFFN programme.
statusRationale: Final splicing works underway; project tracking under budget.

title: CityConnect Cycle Superhighway – Phase 4 A660
description: Extension of segregated cycleway from Headingley to Adel along the A660, linking university campuses to outer suburbs.
statusRationale: Utilities surveys complete; construction starts January 2025.

title: Otley Road Bus Priority & Landscape Scheme
description: Inbound bus lane, upgraded crossings and tree‑line boulevard on A660 from Lawnswood to Weetwood Lane.
statusRationale: Major civils substantially complete; snagging in progress.

title: City Park Affordable Housing Phase 1
description: Construction of 350 affordable apartments adjacent to the new City Park in South Bank.
statusRationale: Contractor procurement ongoing; start on site expected late 2024.

evidence.title: Aire Park Leeds: Housing developer found for “prime plot” on Meadow Lane in the city centre
evidence.summary: Leeds City Council has chosen developer Glenbrook as the preferred bidder for a prime 0.18-hectare plot at Meadow Lane, adjacent to the new City Park (Aire Park) in the South Bank. Glenbrook is expected to seek planning permission to deliver a high-quality residential development of around 350 apartments on the site, with a significant proportion of the homes designated as affordable housing.

evidence.title: £50m secured for Leeds South Bank apartment scheme
evidence.summary: A major housing development in Leeds’s South Bank has secured a £50 million loan from Homes England, enabling construction of 375 apartments across two towers on Water Lane. The project, which also received £4.4 million from West Yorkshire’s Brownfield Housing Fund, commenced building work in May 2024 and is scheduled to complete by early 2027. The scheme supports regional goals for affordable, sustainable homes on brownfield land through a partnership of public and private investment.

title: Brownlee Centre Cycling Circuit Expansion
description: 1 km extension and lighting of the closed‑road cycle circuit at Bodington Playing Fields.
statusRationale: Planning permission granted February 2024; construction underway.

title: East Leeds Orbital Active‑Travel Spine
description: 12 km off‑highway greenway running parallel to ELOR linking new housing sites to cycle network.
statusRationale: Land assembly negotiations ongoing with developers.

title: Leeds Zero‑Carbon School Pilot (3 Sites)
description: Deep‑retrofit of three primary schools with heat pumps, PV and fabric upgrades to achieve net‑zero operations.
statusRationale: First site underway; lesson‑learnt feedback integrated into next two tenders.

title: City Varieties Theatre Heritage Refurbishment
description: Stage machinery replacement, new seating and fabric repairs to the Grade II* music hall.
statusRationale: Works progressing during summer dark period; reopening booked for November 2024.

title: Royal Armouries Museum Expansion & Quay Link
description: Construction of new gallery wing and improved pedestrian link to Leeds Dock water‑taxi stop.
statusRationale: Design competition launched; funding package to be finalised.

title: Calderdale Royal Hospital Redevelopment – Elective Hub
description: Creation of a new elective‑care building to increase surgery capacity as part of the hospitals programme.
statusRationale: Awaiting confirmation of New Hospitals Programme funding slot.

title: Carbon‑Negative Highways Depot (Elland)
description: Construction of a new all‑electric highways depot with solar canopies and battery storage.
statusRationale: Steel frame erected; photovoltaics delivery scheduled July 2024.

title: Kirklees Passivhaus Housing Programme Phase 1
description: Delivery of 180 council homes built to Passivhaus standard across eight brownfield sites.
statusRationale: Planning approvals secured on first three plots; contractor framework in place.

title: Kirklees School Streets Phase 2
description: Implementation of timed traffic restrictions outside 25 primary schools with supporting walking‑bus infrastructure.
statusRationale: Signage and ANPR installation ongoing; launch scheduled September 2024 term.

title: Kirklees Energy‑from‑Waste Plant Life‑Extension
description: Refurbishment of fluidised‑bed boilers and installation of advanced flue‑gas treatment to extend plant life to 2040.
statusRationale: Boiler outage extended due to supply‑chain delays.
